module Users
  module DeleteArticles
    module_function

    def call(user)
      return if user.articles.blank?

      virtual_articles = user.articles.map { |article| Article.new(article.attributes) }
      user.articles.find_each do |article|
        article.reactions.delete_all
        article.comments.includes(:user).find_each do |comment|
          comment.reactions.delete_all
          EdgeCache::BustComment.call(comment)
          EdgeCache::BustUser.call(comment.user)
          comment.delete
        end
        article.discussion_lock&.delete
        article.context_notes.delete_all
        article.article_activity&.delete
        article.trend_memberships.delete_all
        article.profile_pins.delete_all
        article.delete
        article.purge
      end
      virtual_articles.each do |article|
        EdgeCache::BustArticle.call(article)
      end
    end
  end
end

module EdgeCache
  class BustArticle
    TIMEFRAMES = [
      [-> { 1.week.ago }, "week"],
      [-> { 1.month.ago }, "month"],
      [-> { 1.year.ago }, "year"],
      [-> { 5.years.ago }, "infinity"],
    ].freeze
    def self.call(article)
      return unless article

      article.purge
      article.user&.purge
      article.organization&.purge

      cache_bust = EdgeCache::Bust.new

      bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
      bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
      bust_user_profile_pages(article)
    end

    def self.bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
      if article.published_at.to_i > Time.current.to_i
        # TODO
        cache_bust.call("/")
      end

      if article.video.present? && article.published_at.to_i > 10.days.ago.to_i
        cache_bust.call("/videos")
      end

      TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
        next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call)
          .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)

        cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
      end

      if article.published && article.published_at > 1.hour.ago
        cache_bust.call("/latest")
      end

      cache_bust.call("/") if Article.published.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(4).ids.include?(article.id)
    end

    private_class_method :bust_home_pages

    def self.bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
      return unless article.published

      article.tag_list.each do |tag|
        if article.published_at.to_i > 2.minutes.ago.to_i
          cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}/latest")
        end

        TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
          next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call).cached_tagged_with_any(tag)
            .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)

          cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
          12.times do |i|
            cache_bust.call("/api/articles?tag=#{tag}&top=#{i}")
          end
        end

        next unless rand(2) == 1 &&
          Article.published.cached_tagged_with(tag)
            .order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(2).ids.include?(article.id)

        cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}")
      end
    end

    private_class_method :bust_tag_pages

    def self.bust_user_profile_pages(article)
      return unless article.user

      EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
        article.user.profile_cache_keys,
        fallback_paths: article.user.profile_cache_bust_paths,
      )
    end

    private_class_method :bust_user_profile_pages
  end
end

class GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker
  include Sidekiq::Job
  sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace

  def perform(article_id)
    article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
    return unless article && article.published? && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)

    # Lightweight representation: Title, Tags, and first 1000 characters of the body
    text_to_embed = <<~TEXT
      Title: #{article.title}
      Tags: #{article.cached_tag_list}
      Summary: #{article.body_markdown.truncate(1000)}
    TEXT

    client = Ai::Embedding.new(affected_content: article, wrapper: self)
    embedding = client.call(text_to_embed, task_type: "RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT", output_dimensionality: 768)

    if embedding
      article.update_column(:semantic_embedding, embedding)
      UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(article.user_id, article.id)
      Concepts::ClassifyRecordWorker.perform_async("Article", article.id)
    end
  end
end

module Mentions
  # This class creates mentions + associated notifications for Articles and Comments.
  # This class will check to see if there are any @-mentions in the post, and will
  # create the associated mentions inline if necessary.
  class CreateAll
    def self.call(...)
      new(...).call
    end

    def initialize(notifiable)
      @notifiable = notifiable
    end

    def call
      mentioned_users = users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author

      delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(mentioned_users)
      create_mentions_for(mentioned_users)
    end

    private

    def users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
      mentioned_usernames = extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text

      collect_existing_users(mentioned_usernames)
        .then do |existing_mentioned_users|
          reject_notifiable_author(existing_mentioned_users)
        end
    end

    def collect_existing_users(usernames)
      User.registered.where(username: usernames)
    end

    def create_mentions_for(users)
      users.each { |user| create_mention_for(user) }
    end

    def extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
      # The "mentioned-user" css is added by Html::Parser#user_link_if_exists
      doc = Nokogiri::HTML(notifiable.processed_html)

      # Remove any mentions that are embedded within any liquid tags
      non_liquid_tag_mentions = doc.css(".mentioned-user").reject do |tag|
        tag.ancestors(".liquid-comment").any? ||
          tag.ancestors("[class^=ltag]").any? ||
          tag.ancestors("[class*=' ltag']").any?
      end

      non_liquid_tag_mentions.map { |link| link.text.delete("@").downcase }
    end

    def reject_notifiable_author(users)
      users.reject { |user| authored_by?(user, notifiable) }
    end

    def authored_by?(user, notifiable)
      user.id == notifiable.user_id
    end

    def delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(users)
      mentions = notifiable.mentions.where.not(user_id: users).destroy_all
      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: mentions.map(&:id), notifiable_type: "Mention") if mentions.present?
    end

    def user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
      user.notifications.exists?(notifiable_id: notifiable.id, notifiable_type: "Comment")
    end

    def create_mention_for(user)
      # Do not create additional notifications for being mentioned in a comment.
      if notifiable.is_a?(Comment) && user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
        return
      end

      # The mentionable_type is the model that created the mention, the user is the user to be mentioned.
      mention = Mention.create(user_id: user.id, mentionable_id: notifiable.id,
                               mentionable_type: notifiable.class.name)

      # If notifiable is an Article, we need to create the notification for the mention immediately so
      # that the notification exists in the database before we attempt to create other Article-related notifications.
      # However, if notifiable is a Comment, we can create the notification for the mention in the background.
      case notifiable
      when Article
        Notification.send_mention_notification_without_delay(mention)
      when Comment
        Notification.send_mention_notification(mention)
      end

      mention
    end

    attr_reader :notifiable
  end
end

module Articles
  module Feeds
    module FindFeaturedStory
      # @param stories [ActiveRecord::Relation, #detect] pick a
      #        featured story from this enumerable
      # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
      #        story must have a main image
      # @return [Article]
      #
      # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
      #
      # @note One might assume that this would query on the
      #       `Articles.where(featured: true)` but in my sleuthing,
      #       the origin of this method's inner logic never included a
      #       consideration for `featured == true`.  Perhaps that
      #       should change?  This has been reported in
      #       https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/15613 and impacts
      #       Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery
      def self.call(stories, must_have_main_image: true)
        featured_story =
          if must_have_main_image
            if stories.is_a?(ActiveRecord::Relation)
              stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
            else
              stories.detect { |story| story.main_image.present? }
            end
          else
            stories.first
          end

        featured_story || Article.new
      end
    end
  end
end

module Articles
  module Destroyer
    module_function

    def call(article)
      author = article.user

      # comments will automatically lose the connection to their article once `.destroy` is called,
      # due to the `dependent: nullify` clause, so to remove their notifications,
      # we need to cache the ids in advance
      article_comments_ids = article.comments.ids

      article.destroy!

      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article")

      unless article_comments_ids.blank?
        Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article_comments_ids, notifiable_type: "Comment")
      end

      EdgeCache::BustUser.call(author)
    end
  end
end

def destroy
    authorize @article
    Articles::Destroyer.call(@article)
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to "/dashboard", notice: I18n.t("articles_controller.deleted") }
      format.json { head :no_content }
    end
  end

  def stats
    authorize @article
    @organization_id = @article.organization_id
    @reactions = @article.reactions.public_category.order(created_at: :desc).limit(500).includes(:user)
  end

  def admin_unpublish
    authorize @article

    result = Articles::Unpublish.call(current_user, @article)

    if result.success
      Audit::Logger.log(:moderator, current_user, params.dup)
      Note.create(
        noteable: @article.user,
        reason: "unpublish_article",
        content: "#{current_user.username} unpublished post with ID #{@article.id}",
        author: current_user,
      )

      render json: { message: "success", path: @article.current_state_path }, status: :ok

I now have a complete picture. `purge` comes from `Purgeable`, included via `ApplicationRecord` (app/models/application_record.rb:4). Let me compile the full audit.

---

# Article Contract Audit — Forem

A teardown-focused audit of the `Article` model (`app/models/article.rb`), its shared contract, and every dependent. Built from the Sense index (Article is a hub: 177 callers / 503 refs, blast risk **high**, 200 direct production callers, 143 affected files), verified with targeted greps.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Forem — the open-source Rails platform powering dev.to ("where programmers share ideas"). 5,216 files, ~18k symbols, primary language Ruby on Rails, with a Preact/JS frontend and ERB views.

**Layout** (`app/`): standard Rails fan-out plus service-heavy organization — `models/` (+ `models/concerns/`), `controllers/` (+ `controllers/concerns/api/`), `services/` (2,491 symbols — the bulk of business logic, namespaced e.g. `Articles::`, `EdgeCache::`, `Notifications::`), `workers/` (Sidekiq async jobs, namespaced `Articles::`), `queries/` (read models), `liquid_tags/` (embed rendering), `views/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`, `decorators/`. Migrations in `db/migrate`, specs mirror `app/` under `spec/`.

**The central content model** is `Article` at **`app/models/article.rb:1`** (`class Article < ApplicationRecord`). It is what readers publish; comments, reactions, tags, mentions, feed events, notifications, page views, polls, etc. attach to it. It is built on:
- Base class `ApplicationRecord` (`app/models/application_record.rb:1`), which mixes in `Purgeable` (Fastly edge-cache purge) at `:4`.
- Six domain concerns under `app/models/concerns/` (mapped in Step 2).
- Hub neighbors `User` (`app/models/user.rb:1`) and `Organization` (`app/models/organization.rb:1`).

---

## Step 2 — The Article contract and what it's built on

### Shared behavior it pulls in (includes — `app/models/article.rb:2-10`)
| Concern | File:line | What it contributes to the contract |
|---|---|---|
| `Purgeable` (via `ApplicationRecord`) | `app/models/application_record.rb:4` → `app/models/concerns/purgeable.rb:6` | `#purge` / `.purge_all` Fastly edge-cache purge by surrogate key. Called in `before_destroy` and `bust_cache`. |
| `LiquidEmbeddable` | `app/models/concerns/liquid_embeddable.rb:1` | `has_many :liquid_embed_references, dependent: :destroy`; `after_commit` queues `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker`; syncs embed metadata (score/published) on update. |
| `Reactable` | `app/models/concerns/reactable.rb:1` | `has_many :reactions, dependent: :destroy` + `distinct_reaction_categories`; reaction-count sync (`sync_reactions_count`, batch class method). |
| `Taggable` | `app/models/concerns/taggable.rb:1` | `cached_tagged_with` / `cached_tagged_with_any` scopes (feed/tag-page queries depend on these); `before_save :sync_tags_array`; `validate_tag_name`. |
| `UserSubscriptionSourceable` | `app/models/concerns/user_subscription_sourceable.rb:1` | `has_many :user_subscriptions, dependent: :nullify` + `sourced_subscribers`; assumes `user_id`. |
| `WebpageTrackable` | `app/models/concerns/webpage_trackable.rb:1` | `has_many :webpage_references, dependent: :delete_all`; **`before_destroy :capture_webpage_domains, prepend: true`** + `after_destroy_commit :queue_webpage_domain_updates` (linked-domain score recompute on teardown). |
| `AlgoliaSearchable` | `app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable.rb:1` | Mixes in `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle` (`app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb`) — search index sync. |
| `PgSearch::Model` | gem | enables `pg_search_scope :search_articles` (`article.rb:396`). |
| `ActsAsTaggableOn`, `resourcify` (rolify) | `article.rb:12-13` | tag taggings; role authorization. |

### Associations other records hang off it (`app/models/article.rb:44-241` + concerns)
`belongs_to`: `collection` (optional, **no `touch`** — deadlock note `:42`), `organization`, `user`, `subforem`. `counter_culture :user, :organization` (`:50-51`).

`has_many` / `has_one` with **teardown strategy** (the heart of the rework):

| Association | Line | `dependent:` |
|---|---|---|
| `discussion_lock` (has_one) | 168 | `:delete` |
| `mentions` (poly) | 170 | `:delete_all` |
| **`comments` (poly)** | 171 | **`:nullify`** ← comments survive; only the FK is cleared |
| `context_notifications` (poly) | 172 | `:delete_all` |
| `ai_audits` (poly) | 173 | `:nullify` |
| `feed_events` | 176 | `:delete_all` |
| `notification_subscriptions` (poly) | 177 | `:delete_all` |
| `notifications` (poly) | 178 | `:delete_all` |
| `page_views` | 179 | `:delete_all` |
| `article_activity` (has_one) | 180 | `:delete` |
| `polls` | 183 | `:destroy` (cascades votes/options/skips) |
| `profile_pins` (poly) | 184 | `:delete_all` |
| `rating_votes` | 187 | `:destroy` (counter_culture tallies) |
| `tag_adjustments` | 188 | *(none)* |
| `context_notes` | 189 | `:delete_all` |
| `trend_memberships` → `trends` | 192-193 | `:destroy` |
| `concept_memberships` → `concepts` | 195-196 | `:destroy` |
| `reactions` (Reactable) | concern:5 | `:destroy` |
| `user_subscriptions` (UserSubscriptionSourceable) | concern:7 | `:nullify` |
| `liquid_embed_references` (LiquidEmbeddable) | concern:5 | `:destroy` |
| `webpage_references` (WebpageTrackable) | concern:5 | `:delete_all` |
| `top_comments` + 4 score-filtered comment scopes | 198-241 | read-only views |

### What a teammate must know before changing the contract
- **`comments` is `:nullify`, not delete** — so `Articles::Destroyer` (`app/services/articles/destroyer.rb:11`) caches `comment.ids` *before* `destroy!` to remove their `Notification`s, because the FK is gone afterward. Any change to comment teardown must preserve this ordering.
- **Mixed teardown strategies** (`:delete_all` / `:delete` skip callbacks; `:destroy` runs them). `polls` and `rating_votes` are `:destroy` deliberately (cascade + counter_culture); the rest are bulk deletes for speed.
- **`before_destroy_actions, prepend: true`** (`article.rb:313`, def `:1408`) and **`WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains, prepend: true`** both run *before* the dependent rows are gone — order-sensitive.
- Heavy callback surface (Step 3): score, caches, embeddings, social image, org-page recompile, notifications all hang off save/commit/destroy.
- Serialized columns `cached_user` / `cached_organization` (YAML, `:386/:390`) and a Postgres `update_reading_list_document` trigger (`:359`) + `reading_list_document` tsvector are part of the contract.
- Counter columns (`comments_count`, `*reactions_count`, etc.) have presence validations (`:253-267`) — they must stay non-null.

---

## Step 3 — Lifecycle: what fans out on create / update / destroy

### Entry points (where change is triggered)
- **Create:** `Articles::Creator#create_article` (`app/services/articles/creator.rb:69`) → `Article.new(...).save`; `ArticleWithVideoCreationService#create!` (`:9`); `Feeds::Import` (`app/services/feeds/import.rb:142`); `ScheduledAutomations::Executor#create_article` (`:228`).
- **Update:** `ArticlesController#update` → `Articles::Updater`; `Admin::ArticlesController#update` (`:50`); `Articles::Unpublish`; admin/score paths.
- **Destroy:** `ArticlesController#destroy` (`app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:207`) → **`Articles::Destroyer.call`** (`app/services/articles/destroyer.rb:5`); and **`Users::DeleteArticles.call`** (`app/services/users/delete_articles.rb:5`, manual teardown on account deletion); `Organizations::Delete#articles_sync` (`app/services/organizations/delete.rb:35`).

### Callback fan-out (inline vs async), all in `app/models/article.rb`

**Inline — before_validation / before_save / before_create** (`:295-312`): markdown render & `processed_html`/`reading_time` (`evaluate_markdown` 1356), slug, published date, title normalize, BIDI strip, video embed URL, `set_default_subforem_id`, `set_cached_entities` (YAML cache of user/org, 1615), `set_all_dates` (1620), `calculate_base_scores`, `fetch_video_duration` (HTTP, inline 1384), `set_caches` (path, 1265), `detect_language` (1240), `create_password`.

**Inline — after_save** (`:315-319`): `create_conditional_autovomits` → async `Articles::HandleSpamWorker`; **`bust_cache`** → `purge` (Fastly, inline) + `async_bust` → `Articles::BustCacheWorker` + touch author/org `latest_article_updated_at` (1656); `collection_cleanup` (destroys empty collection, inline 1229); `generate_social_image` → async `Images::SocialImageWorker` (1669); `generate_context_notes` → async `Articles::GenerateContextNoteWorker`.

**Async / commit-time** (`:321-345`):
- `after_update_commit :update_notifications` → `Notification.update_notifications` (1400).
- `after_update_commit :update_notification_subscriptions` (on user_id change, 1404).
- `after_commit :enqueue_generate_embedding` → `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker` (1808).
- `after_commit :async_score_calc` → **`Articles::ScoreCalcWorker`** (1122); `:touch_collection`; `:enrich_image_attributes` → `Articles::EnrichImageAttributesWorker`; `:detect_code_block_languages` → `Articles::DetectCodeBlockLanguagesWorker`.
- `after_update_commit :update_dependent_embeds_if_key_info_changed` → `Articles::UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker` (1844).
- `after_update_commit :regenerate_summary_if_content_changed` → `Articles::GenerateSummaryWorker` (1069).
- `after_commit :recompile_organization_pages, on: [create,update,destroy]` → `Organizations::RecompilePagesWorker` (1729).
- `after_commit :bust_cached_admin_welcome_thread` (1766).
- **Concern callbacks:** `LiquidEmbeddable` after_commit → `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker`; `WebpageTrackable` after_commit → `SyncWebpageReferencesWorker`.
- **DB trigger:** `update_reading_list_document` recomputes the tsvector on every insert/update (`:359`, inline in Postgres).

**Score recompute (`update_score`, 972)** itself fans out: `update_columns(score, hotness_score via BlackBox, comment_score, …)` then `trigger_linked_domain_score_updates` → `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker`, `trigger_freeform_context_note_generation`, `trigger_summary_generation`, `trigger_semantic_embedding_generation` → `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker`.

1. `before_destroy :before_destroy_actions, prepend: true` (1408): `bust_cache(destroying: true)` + enqueue `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker` for all sibling article ids of author/org (chunked).

## Step 5 — How article identity and visibility are guarded in background/cross-service work

There are **three distinct guard styles**, and the inconsistency is itself a risk:

1. **Soft find + early return** (dominant, safe against deleted rows): `Article.find_by(id:); return unless article`.
   - `Articles::BustCacheWorker#perform` `bust_cache_worker.rb:4-6`; `Articles::ScoreCalcWorker#perform` `score_calc_worker.rb:8-9`; `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker#perform` `generate_article_embedding_worker.rb:6-7`; most `Articles::*Worker`s follow this.
2. **Soft find + identity *and* visibility check**: `return unless article && article.published? && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)` — `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker:7`. Embedding/summary/context-note triggers gate on `published?`, score thresholds, and column presence in the model itself: `eligible_for_semantic_embedding?` (`article.rb:1026`), `trigger_summary_generation` (`:1049`), `trigger_freeform_context_note_generation` (`:1040`), `enqueue_generate_embedding` (`:1808`), `async_score_calc` returns early `if !published? || destroyed?` (`:1122`).
3. **Hard find (raises) — no guard**: `Images::SocialImageWorker#perform` uses `class_name.constantize.find(id)` (`social_image_worker.rb:8`) — **will raise `RecordNotFound` if the article was torn down before the job runs.** A teardown rework that changes timing must account for this.

**Visibility / currency guards (the "real, current, published" checks):**
- The canonical gate is the **`published` scope** (`article.rb:411`): `where(published: true).where("published_at <= ?", Time.current)` — used everywhere in feeds/sitemaps/caching to exclude drafts and **future-scheduled** posts. `scheduled?` (`:663`) = `published_at.future?`.
- `EdgeCache::BustArticle` guards each branch on `article.published` / `published_at` comparisons (`bust_article.rb:24,40,50,53`) and `article.user`/`article.organization` presence (`:13-14,77-78`) — and operates on **virtual `Article.new` objects** post-delete, so it must tolerate an unsaved record (no `id` row).
- `Articles::Feeds::FindFeaturedStory.call` returns `Article.new` when nothing matches (`find_featured_story.rb:31`) — callers must handle a non-persisted article.
- `skip_indexing?` / `skip_indexing_reason` (`:901/:911`) gate crawler indexing on `published`, score, and date.
- `Mentions::CreateAll` derives identity from `notifiable.user_id` / `notifiable.class.name` and re-parses `processed_html` (`create_all.rb:42,59,79`); it branches on `notifiable.is_a?(Article)` (`:85`) — relies on the article being loaded, not on a freshness re-check.
- `subforem`/`from_subforem` scope (`:428`) gates cross-subforem visibility via `RequestStore`.
- `ExistingPublishedArticleIdValidator` (`existing_published_article_id_validator.rb:2`) validates references point at a still-published article.

Sense blast: **risk = high**, 200 direct production callers, 340 affected symbols, 143 files. The rework changes *how attached data is stored and torn down*, so risk concentrates on the teardown/association layer.

# Article Contract Audit — Forem

A teardown-focused audit of the `Article` model (`app/models/article.rb`), its shared contract, and every dependent. Built from the Sense index (Article is a hub: 177 callers / 503 refs, blast risk **high**, 200 direct production callers, 143 affected files), verified with targeted greps.

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Forem — the open-source Rails platform powering dev.to ("where programmers share ideas"). 5,216 files, ~18k symbols, primary language Ruby on Rails, with a Preact/JS frontend and ERB views.

**Layout** (`app/`): standard Rails fan-out plus service-heavy organization — `models/` (+ `models/concerns/`), `controllers/` (+ `controllers/concerns/api/`), `services/` (2,491 symbols — the bulk of business logic, namespaced e.g. `Articles::`, `EdgeCache::`, `Notifications::`), `workers/` (Sidekiq async jobs, namespaced `Articles::`), `queries/` (read models), `liquid_tags/` (embed rendering), `views/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`, `decorators/`. Migrations in `db/migrate`, specs mirror `app/` under `spec/`.

**The central content model** is `Article` at **`app/models/article.rb:1`** (`class Article < ApplicationRecord`). It is what readers publish; comments, reactions, tags, mentions, feed events, notifications, page views, polls, etc. attach to it. It is built on:
- Base class `ApplicationRecord` (`app/models/application_record.rb:1`), which mixes in `Purgeable` (Fastly edge-cache purge) at `:4`.
- Six domain concerns under `app/models/concerns/` (mapped in Step 2).
- Hub neighbors `User` (`app/models/user.rb:1`) and `Organization` (`app/models/organization.rb:1`).

### What a teammate must know before changing the contract
- **`comments` is `:nullify`, not delete** — so `Articles::Destroyer` (`app/services/articles/destroyer.rb:11`) caches `comment.ids` *before* `destroy!` to remove their `Notification`s, because the FK is gone afterward. Any change to comment teardown must preserve this ordering.
- **Mixed teardown strategies** (`:delete_all` / `:delete` skip callbacks; `:destroy` runs them). `polls` and `rating_votes` are `:destroy` deliberately (cascade + counter_culture); the rest are bulk deletes for speed.
- **`before_destroy_actions, prepend: true`** (`article.rb:313`, def `:1408`) and **`WebpageTrackable#capture_webpage_domains, prepend: true`** both run *before* the dependent rows are gone — order-sensitive.
- Heavy callback surface (Step 3): score, caches, embeddings, social image, org-page recompile, notifications all hang off save/commit/destroy.
- Serialized columns `cached_user` / `cached_organization` (YAML, `:386/:390`) and a Postgres `update_reading_list_document` trigger (`:359`) + `reading_list_document` tsvector are part of the contract.
- Counter columns (`comments_count`, `*reactions_count`, etc.) have presence validations (`:253-267`) — they must stay non-null.

## Step 5 — How article identity and visibility are guarded in background/cross-service work

There are **three distinct guard styles**, and the inconsistency is itself a risk:

1. **Soft find + early return** (dominant, safe against deleted rows): `Article.find_by(id:); return unless article`.
   - `Articles::BustCacheWorker#perform` `bust_cache_worker.rb:4-6`; `Articles::ScoreCalcWorker#perform` `score_calc_worker.rb:8-9`; `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker#perform` `generate_article_embedding_worker.rb:6-7`; most `Articles::*Worker`s follow this.
2. **Soft find + identity *and* visibility check**: `return unless article && article.published? && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)` — `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker:7`. Embedding/summary/context-note triggers gate on `published?`, score thresholds, and column presence in the model itself: `eligible_for_semantic_embedding?` (`article.rb:1026`), `trigger_summary_generation` (`:1049`), `trigger_freeform_context_note_generation` (`:1040`), `enqueue_generate_embedding` (`:1808`), `async_score_calc` returns early `if !published? || destroyed?` (`:1122`).
3. **Hard find (raises) — no guard**: `Images::SocialImageWorker#perform` uses `class_name.constantize.find(id)` (`social_image_worker.rb:8`) — **will raise `RecordNotFound` if the article was torn down before the job runs.** A teardown rework that changes timing must account for this.

**Visibility / currency guards (the "real, current, published" checks):**
- The canonical gate is the **`published` scope** (`article.rb:411`): `where(published: true).where("published_at <= ?", Time.current)` — used everywhere in feeds/sitemaps/caching to exclude drafts and **future-scheduled** posts. `scheduled?` (`:663`) = `published_at.future?`.
- `EdgeCache::BustArticle` guards each branch on `article.published` / `published_at` comparisons (`bust_article.rb:24,40,50,53`) and `article.user`/`article.organization` presence (`:13-14,77-78`) — and operates on **virtual `Article.new` objects** post-delete, so it must tolerate an unsaved record (no `id` row).
- `Articles::Feeds::FindFeaturedStory.call` returns `Article.new` when nothing matches (`find_featured_story.rb:31`) — callers must handle a non-persisted article.
- `skip_indexing?` / `skip_indexing_reason` (`:901/:911`) gate crawler indexing on `published`, score, and date.
- `Mentions::CreateAll` derives identity from `notifiable.user_id` / `notifiable.class.name` and re-parses `processed_html` (`create_all.rb:42,59,79`); it branches on `notifiable.is_a?(Article)` (`:85`) — relies on the article being loaded, not on a freshness re-check.
- `subforem`/`from_subforem` scope (`:428`) gates cross-subforem visibility via `RequestStore`.
- `ExistingPublishedArticleIdValidator` (`existing_published_article_id_validator.rb:2`) validates references point at a still-published article.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (ranked by risk)

Sense blast: **risk = high**, 200 direct production callers, 340 affected symbols, 143 files. The rework changes *how attached data is stored and torn down*, so risk concentrates on the teardown/association layer.

**Critical (will break / data loss if mishandled):**
1. **`Users::DeleteArticles`** (`delete_articles.rb:5`) — hand-rolled teardown that enumerates associations explicitly. Any added/removed/renamed association or changed `dependent:` strategy **silently desyncs here** → orphaned rows or FK errors. Highest-risk because it duplicates the model contract.
2. **`Articles::Destroyer`** (`destroyer.rb:5`) — depends on `comments` being `:nullify` and pre-caches comment ids; changing comment teardown to `:delete_all`/`:destroy` breaks its notification cleanup.
3. **The `dependent:` table** (`article.rb:168-196` + 4 concerns) — `comments :nullify`, `reactions :destroy`, `polls/rating_votes :destroy`, the `:delete_all` set. Each downstream model (Reaction, Comment, Poll, RatingVote, Mention, PageView, FeedEvent, NotificationSubscription, Notification, ContextNote, ProfilePin, TrendMembership, ConceptMembership, UserSubscription, LiquidEmbedReference, WebpageReference) is a dependent.
4. **`before_destroy_actions`** (`:1408`) + **`WebpageTrackable` prepend destroy hooks** (`webpage_trackable.rb:8-9`) — `prepend: true` ordering is load-bearing (they read associations before deletion). `counter_culture :user/:organization` (`:50`) decrements on destroy.

**High (visible breakage):**
5. Cache teardown: `EdgeCache::BustArticle` (virtual-article pattern, `bust_article.rb:9-21`), `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker`, `Purgeable#purge`.
6. `Images::SocialImageWorker#perform` `find(id)` raises on missing row (`:8`) — race with faster teardown.
7. Feed/ranking queries that assume persisted, published articles: `Articles::Feeds::*`, `Homepage::ArticlesQuery`, `EmailDigestArticleCollector`, `PinnedArticle#get`, `Billboard#update_exclude_article_ids`.
8. Notifications/mentions cleanup: `Notification.remove_all` ordering, `Mentions::CreateAll`, `NotificationSubscriptions::*`.
9. Search/embedding sync: `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle`, `pg_search` `reading_list_document` trigger (`:359`), `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker`, `Concepts::ClassifyRecordWorker`.

**Medium:** counter columns w/ presence validations (`:253-267`) must stay non-null; `Organizations::Delete` / org-page recompile; analytics services; serialized `cached_user`/`cached_organization`.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** full destroy path (both `Articles::Destroyer` and `Users::DeleteArticles` + `Organizations::Delete`); no orphaned dependent rows; notification removal still finds comment ids; edge-cache busting still works on a virtual article; social-image worker race; counter-culture decrements; search/embedding cleanup.

**Affected tests flagged by Sense:** `spec/models/article_destroy_spec.rb`, `spec/models/article_spec.rb`, `spec/services/notifications/moderation/send_spec.rb`.
